basics.name: Rachel Kim
basics.label: Family Nurse Practitioner
basics.summary: Board-certified FNP providing primary care and chronic disease management in underserved communities. Passionate about preventive health, patient education, and team-based care models.

work.name: Community Health Partners
work.description: Federally qualified health center serving 25,000 patients annually
work.summary: Full-scope primary care panel of 900 patients across pediatrics, adult, and geriatric populations.
highlights: Improved diabetes HbA1c control rate 18% through group visit programme.
Implemented telehealth workflows during COVID-19 serving 200 visits/week at peak.
Precepts 4 NP students annually; 100% pass board certification on first attempt.

work.name: Northwestern Memorial Hospital
work.description: Academic medical centre – urgent care division
work.summary: Level I trauma centre RN with charge nurse responsibilities on night shift.
highlights: Earned CEN certification within first year.
Served on unit council improving sepsis bundle compliance to 94%.

volunteer.summary: Provide basic primary care and referrals at weekly pop-up clinics for unhoused residents.
highlights: Conducted 1,500+ patient encounters since programme inception.
Coordinated vaccine drives reaching 800 individuals during flu seasons.

awards.title: Excellence in Primary Care
awards.summary: Recognized for outstanding chronic disease management outcomes in FQHC setting.

awards.title: DAISY Award for Extraordinary Nurses
awards.summary: Nominated by patient family for compassionate emergency department care.

certificates.name: Certified Emergency Nurse (CEN)
certificates.issuer: Board of Certification for Emergency Nursing

certificates.name: Illinois APRN License
certificates.issuer: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation

publications.name: Group Visits for Type 2 Diabetes in FQHC Settings
publications.publisher: Journal of the American Association of Nurse Practitioners
publications.summary: Retrospective cohort study of 180 patients showing improved adherence and glycemic control.

projects.name: Diabetes Group Visit Curriculum
projects.description: Structured 12-week shared medical appointment curriculum for Type 2 diabetes.
highlights: Adopted by three CHP clinic sites.
Available in English, Spanish, and Korean patient handouts.

projects.name: NP Preceptor Toolkit
projects.description: Resource pack for clinical preceptors including evaluation rubrics and case log templates.
highlights: Shared with UIC College of Nursing preceptor network.

references.name: Dr. Sandra Ortiz, MD – Medical Director
references.reference: Rachel is the backbone of our primary care team. Her patients trust her, her students learn from her, and her outcomes speak for themselves.

references.name: Michael Torres, RN – Clinic Manager
references.reference: She advocates fiercely for underserved patients while keeping our workflows efficient. An exceptional clinician and colleague.
